zh.lifeSummary: 當Rose Trippe於1552年6月5日出生在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆時，她的父親William Roger Trippe23歲，母親Elizabeth Ann Stone26歲。她於1573年5月14日與Thomas Hammond在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1649年6月27日在她的家鄉享年97歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rose Trippe于1552年6月5日出生在英国素福爾可拉文哈姆时，她的父亲William Roger Trippe23岁，母亲Elizabeth Ann Stone26岁。她于1573年5月14日与Thomas Hammond在英国素福爾可拉文哈姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1649年6月27日在她的家乡享年97岁。

nameMeanings.name: Trippe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Hampshire and Somerset) or German: variant of   Tripp  . This form of the English name is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Tripp
meanings: English: from Middle English trip(pe) (n) ‘(to) dance; stumble’ (Old French tripe(r)), either an occupational name for a dancer, or a nickname for someone who had difficulty walking.
English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English trip(e) ‘tripe’ (Old French tripe).
German: metonymic occupational name for a maker of wooden pattens (trippe), a type of raised sole that could be strapped to normal footwear for walking in unpaved muddy streets.

nameMeanings.name: Rose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Ostensibly from the vocabulary word denoting the flower (Latin rosa). However, the name was in use throughout the Middle Ages, long before any of the other girls' names derived from flowers, which are generally of 19th-century origin. In part it may refer to the flower as a symbol of the Virgin Mary, but it seems more likely that it also has a Germanic origin, probably as a short form of various girls' names based on hros ‘horse’ or hrōd ‘fame’. The Latinate form Rohesia is commonly found in documents of the Middle Ages. As well as being a name in its own right, it is currently used as a short form of Rosemary and, less often (because of their different pronunciation), of other names beginning Ros-, such as Rosalind and Rosamund .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Rose Trippe was born on 5 June 1552, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, her father, William Roger Trippe, was 23 and her mother, Elizabeth Ann Stone, was 26. She married Thomas Hammond on 14 May 1573, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. She died about 27 June 1649, in her hometown, at the age of 97.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rose Trippe est née le 5 juin 1552, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, William Roger Trippe, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Ann Stone, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Hammond le 14 mai 1573, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée environ 27 juin 1649, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 97 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rose Trippe am 5. Juni 1552 in Lavenham, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Roger Trippe 23 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Ann Stone 26. Sie hat am 14. Mai 1573 in Lavenham, Suffolk, England Thomas Hammond geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb um 27. Juni 1649 im Alter von 97 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rose Trippe è nata il il 5 giugno 1552 nel Lavenham, Suffolk, England, sua padre, William Roger Trippe, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Ann Stone, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Hammond il 14 maggio 1573, nel Lavenham, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Morì circa 27 giugno 1649, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 97 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Rose Trippeが1552年6月5日にイギリススフォルクラベンハムで生まれた時，父William Roger Trippeは23歳，母Elizabeth Ann Stoneは26歳であった。1573年5月14日にイギリススフォルクラベンハムでThomas Hammondと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1649年6月27日に故郷で97歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Rose Trippe(이)가 1552년 6월 5일 영국 수폴크 레이븐함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Roger Trippe(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Ann Stone(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1573년 5월 14일 영국 수폴크 레이븐함에서 Thomas Hammond(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1649년 6월 27일 그녀의 고향에서 97세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.5 June 1552–about 27 June 1649: 5 de junho de 1552–aproximadamente 27 de junho de 1649
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rose Trippe nasceu em 5 de junho de 1552, em Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Roger Trippe, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Ann Stone, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Hammond em 14 de maio de 1573, em Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 27 de junho de 1649, em sua cidade natal, com 97 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rose Trippe родилась 5 июня 1552 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William Roger Trippe было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Ann Stone, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Hammond 14 мая 1573 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла около 27 июня 1649 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 97 лет.

Murió aproximadamente 27 de junio de 1649, en su pueblo, a la edad de 97 años.
